refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall automatically derive performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from worksite mission context inputs, record them in a traceable baseline, and maintain a managed history of all requirement updates and calibration changes with full audit trail.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was vague on 'method' and lacked explicit traceability and history management. Refinement adds specificity: (1) automatic derivation from mission context, (2) baseline recording with traceability, (3) managed history with audit trail. This aligns with acceptance criteria demanding documented performance targets traceable to worksite inputs and maintained baselines/calibration history.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify by: (1) providing a worksite mission context input (e.g., site layout, production target, safety constraints), (2) confirming system derives and records performance requirements, (3) inspecting baseline documentation for traceability links, (4) validating audit trail captures all updates with timestamp, user, and change rationale.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: The system shall accept worksite mission context inputs (site parameters, production targets, safety policies, equipment constraints).
CR-NGHE-005.2: The system shall algorithmically derive performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from mission context.
CR-NGHE-005.3: The system shall record derived requirements in a persistent baseline with full traceability to source mission inputs.
CR-NGHE-005.4: The system shall maintain a managed history of all requirement and calibration updates with audit trail (timestamp, user, change rationale).
CR-NGHE-005.5: The system shall provide query and reporting capability to retrieve historical baselines and track requirement evolution.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized Performance Derivation Engine – A dedicated algorithmic module that ingests mission context, applies derivation rules (heuristic or model-based), and outputs performance targets to a persistent baseline store with versioning.

DP-B: Distributed Derivation with Local Caching – Performance derivation logic embedded in Vehicle and Teleoperation modules, each caching local baselines; periodic synchronization to a central registry.
DP-C: Manual Requirement Entry with Validation – Site Manager manually enters performance targets via UI; system validates against mission context and stores with audit trail.

DP-D: Hybrid Derivation with Human-in-the-Loop – Automated derivation engine proposes targets; Site Manager reviews, adjusts, and approves; system records both proposed and approved baselines with full audit trail.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-D: Hybrid Derivation with Human-in-the-Loop – Automated derivation engine proposes performance targets from mission context; Site Manager reviews, adjusts, and approves; system records both proposed and approved baselines with full audit trail and traceability.

potential_sources: DP-D couples performance derivation (algorithmic) with human approval (governance). This is intentional and justified: high safety criticality demands human oversight; hybrid approach balances automation efficiency with stakeholder accountability.
DP-D requires bidirectional coupling with mission context input (FR-005.1) and baseline storage/history (FR-005.3, FR-005.4). These are functional dependencies, not design flaws.
No unintended cross-domain coupling detected. Derivation logic remains independent of Vehicle or Teleoperation operational logic.
coupling_check.notes: Hybrid approach is appropriate for high safety criticality. Decoupling risk is low if derivation engine and approval workflow are cleanly separated in implementation.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Site Manager has sufficient domain expertise to review and adjust derived targets. RISK: If Site Manager lacks expertise, approval may be ineffective. MITIGATION: Provide decision support (e.g., rationale for each derived target, comparison to historical baselines).

ASSUMPTION: Mission context inputs are complete and accurate. RISK: Incomplete or incorrect inputs lead to invalid derived targets. MITIGATION: Implement input validation and completeness checks before derivation.

ASSUMPTION: Derivation algorithm is deterministic and reproducible. RISK: Non-deterministic or opaque algorithms undermine traceability and auditability. MITIGATION: Document derivation rules explicitly; log all intermediate steps.

RISK: High safety criticality demands that approval workflow cannot be bypassed. MITIGATION: Enforce mandatory approval gate in system logic; prevent unapproved baselines from being used operationally.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Strengthen DP-D by explicitly specifying: (1) derivation algorithm (rule-based, model-based, or hybrid), (2) approval workflow (single or multi-level review), (3) audit trail schema (what metadata is captured), (4) fallback behavior if derivation fails or approval is withheld. These details will be refined in sub-FR decomposition.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the top-level system boundary. It owns the performance requirement derivation and baseline management capability, integrating mission context inputs, derivation logic, approval workflow, and persistent baseline storage. This module coordinates with Vehicle (for operational constraints), Teleoperation (for remote mission context), and Safety Services (for safety-critical approval gates).

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives mission context inputs (site parameters, production targets, equipment constraints, safety policies) from Site Manager or Tele-operator via Tasking interface.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Outputs derived performance requirements and baseline records (cycle time targets, energy efficiency targets, availability targets, safety margins) to Vehicle and Teleoperation for operational use.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Coordinates with Safety Services via SafetyAlert interface: (1) receives safety policy constraints and approval authority, (2) sends derived safety margins and baseline changes for safety review and approval.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Uses Network Link for persistent baseline storage (cloud or on-site database), audit trail logging, and historical baseline retrieval. Enables Site Manager to query and report on requirement evolution.

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the natural top-level owner. Performance requirement derivation and baseline management are system-level concerns that must be coordinated across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services. Containment is high because the derivation engine, approval workflow, and baseline store are logically cohesive and must be centrally managed for traceability and auditability. Minimal diffusion: only external interfaces to mission context (Tasking), operational use (Telemetry), safety approval (SafetyAlert), and storage (Network Link).

Vehicle module is responsible for operational execution, not requirement derivation. Assigning DP-D to Vehicle would scatter derivation logic across the vehicle control stack, breaking traceability and making audit trail management difficult. Vehicle should consume derived requirements (via Telemetry), not own their derivation. High diffusion risk.

Teleoperation module is responsible for remote mission control and operator interface, not requirement derivation. While Tele-operator may provide mission context inputs, the derivation logic and baseline management must remain centralized in NGHE Solution for consistency and auditability. Assigning to Teleoperation would create coupling between operator interface and system-level requirement management. High diffusion risk.

Safety Services is responsible for safety monitoring and alerts, not requirement derivation. While Safety Services must approve derived safety margins (via SafetyAlert interface), the derivation engine itself should not be embedded in Safety Services. This would conflate safety approval with safety derivation, violating separation of concerns. High diffusion risk.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the correct owner because: (1) Performance requirement derivation is a system-level concern that must be coordinated across all operational modules (Vehicle, Teleoperation, Safety Services). (2) Baseline management and audit trail are system-level governance functions. (3) Centralizing derivation in NGHE Solution ensures traceability, auditability, and consistency. (4) External interfaces (Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Network Link) are all system-level integration points. (5) High safety criticality demands centralized control and oversight, not distributed logic.
containment_risks: RISK: NGHE Solution module may become a 'god object' if too many system-level concerns are assigned to it. MITIGATION: Decompose NGHE Solution into sub-modules (e.g., Performance Derivation Engine, Baseline Manager, Approval Workflow) in the next decomposition level.

RISK: Tight coupling between derivation logic and approval workflow may make it difficult to change approval policies without modifying derivation code. MITIGATION: Implement approval workflow as a configurable policy engine, decoupled from derivation logic.

RISK: Dependency on Network Link for persistent storage may create a single point of failure for baseline management. MITIGATION: Implement redundancy and failover mechanisms in baseline storage (e.g., local cache with eventual consistency to central store).
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Confirm that NGHE Solution module has sufficient architectural scope to own performance requirement derivation and baseline management. If NGHE Solution is already decomposed into sub-modules (e.g., Performance Derivation Engine, Baseline Manager), assign DP-D to the appropriate sub-module and document the internal interface contracts. Ensure that the Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, and Network Link interfaces are formally defined with schema and protocol specifications.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-D (Hybrid Derivation with Human-in-the-Loop) directly addresses CR-NGHE-005 Refined. The DP specifies: (1) automated derivation from mission context (addresses FR-005.2), (2) baseline recording with traceability (addresses FR-005.3), (3) managed history with audit trail (addresses FR-005.4), (4) human approval for high safety criticality (addresses acceptance criteria and risk tailoring). No functional gaps detected. Coupling is intentional and justified.

diffusion_risks: DIFFUSION RISK (Low): DP-D requires integration with Vehicle and Teleoperation modules to consume derived requirements. This is expected and managed via Telemetry interface. No unintended diffusion of derivation logic into Vehicle or Teleoperation.

DIFFUSION RISK (Low): DP-D requires integration with Safety Services for approval of safety margins. This is expected and managed via SafetyAlert interface. No unintended diffusion of safety approval logic into NGHE Solution core.
DIFFUSION RISK (Low): DP-D requires persistent storage via Network Link. This is expected and managed via a clean interface. No unintended diffusion of storage logic into NGHE Solution core.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the correct owner. Containment score of 95 indicates high cohesion and minimal diffusion. All external interfaces are system-level integration points, not internal module boundaries. Ownership is clear and unambiguous.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: CR-NGHE-005 Refined is verifiable at the NGHE Solution level. Verification approach: (1) provide a worksite mission context input via Tasking interface, (2) confirm NGHE Solution derives and records performance requirements, (3) inspect baseline documentation for traceability to mission inputs, (4) validate audit trail captures all updates. Verification is feasible with standard testing and inspection methods.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear. CR-NGHE-005 is allocated to NGHE Solution module. Sub-FRs (CR-NGHE-005.1 through CR-NGHE-005.5) will be allocated to sub-modules in the next decomposition level (e.g., Mission Context Ingestion, Derivation Engine, Baseline Store, Audit Trail Manager, Query/Reporting Engine).

recommended_revisions: Refine DP-D to specify: (1) derivation algorithm (rule-based, model-based, or hybrid), (2) approval workflow (single or multi-level review, approval authority), (3) audit trail schema (metadata captured, retention policy), (4) fallback behavior if derivation fails or approval is withheld.

Define formal interface specifications for Tasking (mission context input schema), Telemetry (performance requirement output schema), SafetyAlert (safety approval protocol), and Network Link (baseline storage schema).
Establish traceability matrix linking CR-NGHE-005 Refined, sub-FRs, DP-D, and NGHE Solution module. Ensure all acceptance criteria are mapped to verification methods.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-005 Refined is not atomic. It decomposes into five sub-FRs (CR-NGHE-005.1 through CR-NGHE-005.5) that address distinct functional concerns: mission context ingestion, derivation logic, baseline recording, history management, and query/reporting. Each sub-FR requires its own FR -> DP -> IM decomposition to ensure complete coverage and clear ownership. Proceeding to the next decomposition level is necessary to allocate sub-FRs to sub-modules and refine DP-D into concrete design decisions.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: The system shall accept worksite mission context inputs (site parameters, production targets, safety policies, equipment constraints).
CR-NGHE-005.2: The system shall algorithmically derive performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from mission context.
CR-NGHE-005.3: The system shall record derived requirements in a persistent baseline with full traceability to source mission inputs.
CR-NGHE-005.4: The system shall maintain a managed history of all requirement and calibration updates with audit trail (timestamp, user, change rationale).
CR-NGHE-005.5: The system shall provide query and reporting capability to retrieve historical baselines and track requirement evolution.

assumptions: Site Manager has sufficient domain expertise to review and adjust derived performance targets.
Mission context inputs are complete and accurate.
Derivation algorithm is deterministic and reproducible.
High safety criticality demands mandatory human approval; approval workflow cannot be bypassed.
NGHE Solution module has sufficient architectural scope to own performance requirement derivation and baseline management.
Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, and Network Link interfaces are available and can be extended to support performance requirement derivation.
open_risks: RISK: If Site Manager lacks expertise, approval may be ineffective. MITIGATION: Provide decision support (e.g., rationale for each derived target, comparison to historical baselines).
RISK: Incomplete or incorrect mission context inputs lead to invalid derived targets. MITIGATION: Implement input validation and completeness checks before derivation.
RISK: Non-deterministic or opaque derivation algorithms undermine traceability and auditability. MITIGATION: Document derivation rules explicitly; log all intermediate steps.
RISK: NGHE Solution module may become a 'god object' if too many system-level concerns are assigned to it. MITIGATION: Decompose NGHE Solution into sub-modules in the next decomposition level.
RISK: Tight coupling between derivation logic and approval workflow may make it difficult to change approval policies. MITIGATION: Implement approval workflow as a configurable policy engine.
RISK: Dependency on Network Link for persistent storage may create a single point of failure. MITIGATION: Implement redundancy and failover mechanisms in baseline storage.
evidence_needed: Formal specification of derivation algorithm (rule-based, model-based, or hybrid).
Approval workflow specification (single or multi-level review, approval authority, escalation procedures).
Audit trail schema specification (metadata captured, retention policy, query interface).
Fallback behavior specification (what happens if derivation fails or approval is withheld).
Interface specifications for Tasking, Telemetry, SafetyAlert, and Network Link (schema, protocol, error handling).
Traceability matrix linking CR-NGHE-005 Refined, sub-FRs, DP-D, and NGHE Solution module.
Verification plan for CR-NGHE-005 Refined (test cases, inspection criteria, acceptance criteria).
Risk assessment for high safety criticality (failure modes, mitigation strategies, residual risk).
